commit.message:
Fix stale localStorage identity causing join page redirect loop

- Boot useEffect now validates saved myPhone against DB; if the account
  was deleted, clears localStorage and shows the identity selection screen
- Join page no longer auto-redirects to home when localStorage has a
  myPhone value, so users with stale/wrong identities can still register

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite voice input to accumulate finals across session restarts

Mobile Chrome ends recognition sessions after a few seconds of silence,
even with continuous=true. The previous approach rebuilt the full
transcript from event.results on each onresult, but when the session
restarted, event.results reset to empty and all previous text was lost
or duplicated.

New approach uses three refs:
- baseTextRef: text that existed before voice was toggled on
- accumulatedFinalsRef: locked-in finals from all previous sessions
- sessionFinalsRef: finals from the current session

On each onresult, display = base + accumulated + session finals + interim.
On onend before restart, session finals move into accumulated pool.
This prevents both the overwrite bug and the stutter/duplication bug.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: background remote runner correctly so SSH returns immediately

gcloud compute ssh --command blocks until all SSH session processes exit,
even with & at the end. Wrapping in a subshell + disown detaches the
nohup process from the shell so SSH exits straight after launch.

Without this fix, the SSH connection for launch_remote_runner blocked
for the full engine duration (63 min), returned non-zero when nohup
completed, and the launcher incorrectly marked the run as
remote_start_failed — losing results when a SPOT VM was preempted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Every package now has its methods properly indexed.

- exampleTick uses sendRich with device topic for watchOS support

to PushConfig.topic when not set.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: auto-match windows by name + per-window severity verdicts

Automatically pairs investigation windows with the same name across
both sessions and computes per-window regression scores.

On load:
  Matched window pairs (Y/U to navigate):
    [stress onset] avg score: 72.3 [SEVERE]
    [recovery] avg score: 18.1 [MODERATE]

Sorted worst-first so the most severe regression is always first.

Controls:
- Y: jump to next matched window pair (sets both sides + recalculates)
- U: jump to previous matched window pair

Implementation:
- buildWindowMatches() finds name-identical windows across sessions
- computeMatchedWindowScore() averages per-frame regression scores
  over the aligned window range
- applyMatch() sets both window indices, resets to frame 0, rebuilds
  anomaly index for the new window range

feat: hybrid scoring with geometric mean, advisory hard cap, and critical floor

Replace arithmetic weighted average with a hybrid scoring strategy that
prevents a single critical weakness from being masked by strong scores
elsewhere. Three mechanisms: (1) weighted geometric mean of pillar scores
naturally penalizes low outliers, (2) Level 4 "Do Not Travel" advisory
hard-caps score at 2/10, (3) critical floor caps score when any pillar
falls below 0.25. All 568 historical snapshots regenerated with v6.0.0.

commit.message:
feat: add multi-window compare mode

Compare named investigation windows across sessions instead of only
full sessions. Answers "how does stress onset in baseline compare to
stress onset in regression?"

Window selection:
- W: cycle left side through windows (full session, then each window)
- Q: cycle right side through windows
- Alignment: relative frame index within each window (frame 0 = start)

Behavior:
- Side labels show window name instead of scenario
- maxIndex recalculates to window length
- Anomaly index rebuilds for the selected window range
- Score/diff/heatmap reflect window-only data
- All existing controls (play, step, range, export) work within windows

Console output:
  Window: Left=[stress onset] Right=[stress onset]

Available windows listed at startup from both session metadata files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix OOM on bulk upload — stream to disk, limit concurrency to 2

Media upload was reading entire files into memory (await file.read()),
causing OOM when uploading multiple large audio files simultaneously.
Now streams to SpooledTemporaryFile (spills to disk at 2MB) and checks
size incrementally. Frontend batches uploads 2 at a time instead of all
at once.

  "Tick 842, frame=14.2ms, budget=85%. Alerts: engine.frameBudgetHigh(WARNING). Events: 12"

Notes are:
- Printed to console when jumping to bookmarks (J) or windows (;/')
- Saved to meta.json alongside labels
- Restored on next session load

This makes session bundles self-explanatory when reopened days later.

Fix wholesale save wiping project data: accumulate debounced assignments

The persistAssignments debounce was replacing pending assignments on each
call instead of accumulating them. When multiple setActiveLibraryItem calls
happened within 500ms (e.g. saving wholesale + auto-assigning), only the
last call's assignment was sent to the backend, effectively dropping the
earlier ones. Now uses pendingAssignmentsRef to merge all pending
assignments before flushing to the API.

Add memory leak bug report with reproduction script

CandidateResolver.state_ and StateResolver.state_cache_/finalized_blocks_
maps grow unboundedly - entries never cleaned up after finalization.
Demonstrated ~170-245 KB/block growth rate, projecting to 12-20 GB/day.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: rewrite voice input to create fresh recognition per session

Previous implementation created one SpeechRecognition instance in a
useEffect and reused it, causing stale closure issues with the
finalTranscript variable and failures when restarting after auto-stop.

New approach:
- Creates a fresh SpeechRecognition instance each time the user clicks
  the mic button, avoiding stale state
- Captures current textarea value before listening so speech appends
  to existing text instead of replacing it
- Iterates all results (not just from resultIndex) to build the
  complete transcript
- try/catch around recognition.start() for permission errors
- Cleans up recognition reference on end/error

repository.name: shopsalida

commit.message:
Use multi-dimensional embedding geometry to drive optimization

Adds embedding_metrics.py which computes cluster quality and intrinsic
dimensionality on the raw (pre-t-SNE) fc1 embeddings after each run:

Metrics computed:
- Silhouette score — how well-separated classes are in representation space
- Separation ratio — inter-class distance / intra-class distance
- Intrinsic dimensionality — how many PCA dims explain 90%/99% variance
- Per-class intra-cluster distance — which classes the model struggles with
- Davies-Bouldin, Calinski-Harabasz scores
- Worst-separated class pair

Analyzer now emits findings from these metrics:
- Poor silhouette → suggest increasing conv_channels / fc_size
- Low intrinsic dimensionality → model over-parameterized, shrink fc_size
- High intrinsic dimensionality → fc_size too small

Tuner responds to embedding findings by adjusting architecture config
(conv_channels, fc_size) across iterations, not just lr/dropout.

Run detail page shows a new Embedding Quality card with inline bar
charts for per-class compactness and a summary stats table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix t-SNE for 4D/5D by switching to exact method

sklearn's barnes_hut t-SNE algorithm only supports n_components <= 3.
Use method='exact' for dims > 3. Exact is slower but correct.
